body_txt: So, two characters are to be paired up. But there's still something missing. Perhaps they've had too little screen time and chemistry and the audience might feel they are Strangled by the Red String . Or perhaps the author wants to include a fluffy episode with kids but the couple hasn't progressed to having their own babies yet. The perfect solution is for one of the characters to have a much younger sibling, and the other one to act as Cool Big Sis or Big Brother Mentor (or Parental Substitute , in some cases). If there is a Love Triangle underway, expect the kid to bond with the one who'll be victorious, and if there are troubles with the romance, the kid will play Shipper on Deck . The trope can be played two ways: either the friendship with the younger sibling will happen before the romance with the elder (and can be a factor in triggering it), or it will happen with the romance underway already. This trope can also happen with more distant co -siblings-in-law (the sibling of one's brother or sister-in-law; also called "concuño/a" in Spanish), though that is less common unless the families are close with each other. Same thing with step-siblings-in-law, cousins-in-law, and so on. Compare Best Friends-in-Law for friends becoming in-laws, and Good Stepmother for a stepparent filling this role. Contrast My Sister Is Off-Limits for a usually older sibling disapproving of the character's relationship. Sister Trope to Winning Over the Kids , where the kids being befriended are the actual children of the love interest instead of their sibling. May involve Practically Different Generations and Intergenerational Friendship .
